document.title: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: MDR Annex XIV, Part B – Post-market clinical follow-up
MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020
MDCG 2020-8 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Evaluation Report Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020

sourceSummary.oneLine: PMCF Evaluation Report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR context.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents PMCF activities, clinical data findings, equivalent or similar devices, impact on technical documentation, and applicable MDR/MDCG references for clinical evaluation and technical documentation updates.
keyPoints: The report states PMCF is a continuous process that updates the clinical evaluation and is addressed in the manufacturer's post-market surveillance plan.
The devices are digital flat panel X-ray detectors intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, except infants.
The device is classified as Class IIa in accordance with rule 17 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The report states there were no new critical issues regarding performance or safety at this PMCF.
The report references A-TCF-5-118 Clinical Evaluation Report (Rev.1) and A-TCF-5-104 Risk Management Report (Rev.2).

evidence.claim: The detectors are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors designed for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The detector consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics that convert read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Technology

evidence.claim: The detectors support auto exposure detection technology allowing use without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: Intended users include radiologists and medical imaging specialists.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience Radiologist, medical imaging specialists
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Users

evidence.evidenceText: The total points of table D1 and D2 are 21 points so this clinical data is sufficient for scientific Literature databases.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present as a real date; source contains placeholders such as '날짜' and 'Reported date of the first report: 20XX.XX.XX'.
Several PMCF results sections contain template instructions or placeholders rather than completed findings, including survey results and collected data counts marked as X.
MHRA recall evidence is internally inconsistent: one table states 0 results, while a later appraisal table states 'No. of Recall 10' before also stating no MHRA recall cases.
Authority context is primarily CE/MDR, but the document also references FDA, MFDS, and MHRA as field data sources rather than issuing authorities for the document.
Some extracted text includes bilingual Korean template prompts and imperfect wording, indicating the source may be a draft or partially completed template.
